reasoning: The Lean statement encodes the average number of local maxima correctly. The local-maximum condition `(k.1 = 0 ∨ pnat p (k-1) < pnat p k) ∧ (k = n-1 ∨ pnat p k > pnat p (k+1))` correctly handles all three informal cases: at k=0 (informal k=1) only the right-comparison matters; at k=n-1 (informal k=n) only the left-comparison matters; for interior k both comparisons are required. The `pnat` wrapper is used to safely extend the permutation to ℕ→ℕ so that `k-1` and `k+1` can be evaluated, and boundary disjunctions prevent any issue when these fall outside Fin n. The denominator `{p | true}.ncard` equals n! (the total number of permutations). Because the RHS has type ℝ, the division elaborates as real division (Lean coerces the ℕ-valued sum and ncard to ℝ), giving the true rational average — this is necessary for the solution (n+1)/3 to be achievable, e.g., for n=3 where the true average is 4/3 (not the Nat-division result 1). The encoding of "for all permutations" via Fintype's Equiv.Perm (Fin n) and Finset.sum matches the informal "averaging over all permutations of S". The hypothesis n > 1 matches the informal "n > 1".
